description: Set up the i18n infrastructure with react-i18next, create English and Simplified Chinese translation files, extract all user-facing strings from main.tsx, add a language switcher in the sidebar, persist language preference in localStorage, and default to English.
steps: Run npm install in companion/ to install existing dependencies, then install i18next, react-i18next, and i18next-browser-languagedetector as production dependencies

Create companion/src/i18n/ directory with the following files: (a) index.ts - i18next initialization config with localStorage persistence and English as default/fallback language, (b) en.json - English translation file with all extracted strings, (c) zh-CN.json - Simplified Chinese translation file with all translated strings

In the i18n/index.ts initialization: use i18next.init with resources loaded from the JSON files, set fallbackLng to 'en', set lng to localStorage value or 'en', configure the languagedetector plugin to check localStorage first, set interpolation.escapeValue to false (React already escapes)

Extract ALL user-facing strings from companion/src/main.tsx into translation keys organized by view/section. This includes: sidebar labels (DEVICE, VOICE, FLASH, LOGS), brand text ('Ki-board', 'Go Talk'), topbar text ('USB CDC...', view titles), status text ('BOARD ONLINE'/'BOARD OFFLINE'), LCD labels (LINK, FIRMWARE, VOICE STATUS, VOICE MODE), all button labels (Connect, Disconnect, Refresh, Pair, etc.), all hint/description text, form labels, select options, error messages, pair dialog text, flash panel text, voice panel text, logs panel text, footer text

In main.tsx: import the i18n config (import './i18n') and import { useTranslation } from 'react-i18next'. In the App component, call const { t } = useTranslation() and replace all hardcoded strings with t('key') calls. For the viewTitle() function and other helpers outside the component, either move them inside or use i18next.t directly

Add a language switcher UI in the sidebar (between the nav buttons and the footer). It should show the current language and allow toggling between English and Chinese. Use a simple button or select element styled consistently with existing nav buttons. When switched, call i18n.changeLanguage(lng) which automatically persists to localStorage via the detector plugin
Ensure the tsconfig.json has resolveJsonModule: true (already set) so translation JSON imports work correctly
acceptance_criteria: npm run typecheck passes with no errors
npm run frontend:build succeeds and produces output in dist/
All visible text in the UI comes from translation files (no hardcoded user-facing strings remain in JSX)
The app defaults to English when no language preference is stored
Switching to zh-CN updates all UI text to Simplified Chinese without page reload
Language preference persists in localStorage and is restored on app reload
A language switcher control is visible in the sidebar
verification: Verify no hardcoded English UI strings remain in main.tsx JSX by checking that all text in JSX uses t() calls
findings: All hardcoded strings successfully extracted from main.tsx into en.json and zh-CN.json. Review issues addressed: (1) dangerouslySetInnerHTML replaced with Trans component for device.pairHint and pairModal.matchHint, (2) pairMessage state now stores { key, params } objects translated at render time instead of pre-rendered strings, (3) language switcher uses i18n.resolvedLanguage for active state detection to handle region-qualified codes, (4) version string uses APP_VERSION constant with i18next interpolation (v{{version}}) to avoid duplication.
